characters.name: Ada Coy
characters.description: Ada Coy was the character admitted to theElysian Park Garden Clubinstead ofAlma Fillcot. She was voted in asRita Castillocalled the members imploring them to change their votes from Alma to Ada.

characters.name: Alma Fillcot
characters.description: Alma FillcotBiographical InformationAgeEarly 40sGenderFemaleDate of Birth1908-1909StatusDeceasedDate of Death1949-1950Cause of DeathCapital Punishment(Execution)Character InformationOccupationHousewifeLeader of the Garden ClubRelationshipsFamilyBertram Fillcot(husband) †Dee Fillcot(daughter)Vern Loomis(son-in-law)Unnamed GrandchildFriendsGrace Berk(former)Rita Castillo(briefly)EnemiesRita Castillo†Vern LoomisJoanPortrayalPortrayerAllison TolmanSeasons2First AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Lady ConfessesAlma Fillcot is one of the main characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byAllison Tolman. She is a timid and awkward housewife, who remains optimistic in the face of the world’s cruelty. Alma yearns for a spot in the local garden club and to see her ungainly daughter married, but her life is disrupted once she learns of her husband’s secret hobby. Bertram: "Shall I answer it?"Alma: "And say what? Mrs. Yost can't come to the phone, she's buried beneath my begonias?"

characters.name: Amy Grove
characters.description: Amy GroveBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilySimone Grove(mother)Karl Grove(stepfather)RomancesBrad Jenkins(fiancé)PortrayalPortrayerLi Jun LiSeasonsSeason 1First AppearanceYou Had Me at HomicideAmy Groveis one of the recurring characters onCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byLi Jun Li. Simone’s privileged daughter who lives life full throttle. Amy is shattered by her fiancé’s past indiscretion. Will she come to her senses or will a cigarette, a bottle of wine and the cute younger guy down the street change things? Amy and Brad had an on and off relationship. Despite that, they were set to get married, until she discovers he slept with his ex-girlfriend, Cindy, two years prior. She asksTommy Harteout for some cheering up, and to seduce him to pay Brad back. Later, Brad shows up at the restaurant to talk to her; they kiss outside the restaurant and continue with the wedding.

characters.name: April Warner
characters.description: April WarnerBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationSingerDiner Waitress (formerly)RelationshipsFamilyUnnamed FatherUnnamed MotherElsie Warner(daughter)RomancesRob Stanton(affair)FriendsBeth Ann StantonPortrayalPortrayerSadie CalvanoSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeApril Warneris one of the main characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed bySadie Calvano. She is a beautiful young European-American waitress with a free spirit. She’s a burgeoning 1960’s flower child who strikes up an unusual friendship with a1960s housewife. April grew up in a complicated household. Her father was a doctor, who hit her mother. She would watch her mother apply makeup to cover up her black eyes, and smear green concealer to cover the bruise, and apply a second layer to match her skin tone. Everyone in her family knew what her father was doing, but did nothing to stop it. From there, she began to look out for others and lend a helping hand to those in need.

characters.name: Bertram Fillcot
characters.description: Bertram FillcotBiographical InformationAgemid 40s-50sGenderMaleDate of Birth1908-1909StatusDeceasedDate of DeathDecember 3rd, 1949Cause of DeathSuicideCharacter InformationOccupationVeterinarianSerial Killer (formerly)EducationGraduate DegreeNicknameBertieDocRelationshipsFamilyAlma Fillcot(wife) †Dee Fillcot(daughter)Vern Loomis(son-in-law)Unnamed Mother †Unnamed GrandchildRomancesAlma Fillcot(wife) †PortrayalPortrayerNick FrostJack Seavor McDonald(Young Bertram)SeasonsSeason 2First AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Lady ConfessesBertram Fillcotis one of the main characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byNick Frost. Alma’s beloved husband who spends his days as a veterinarian putting sick and injured animals out of their misery. Always kind and good-humoured, Bertram has a secret hobby that is darker than his jovial nature lets on. When he was a child, his mother was very ill. She asked him to kill her to end her suffering, reassuring him he was doing a good thing. He later confessed this to his priest, who again told him he was helping her and that it wasn't his fault. This incident warped his mind into believing it was his duty to 'help' anyone in a great amount of pain, and he grew into a serial killer, killing dozens of people before the start of the series and keeping a labelled memento from each of them "to remember them by"

characters.name: Beth Ann Stanton
characters.description: Beth Ann StantonBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationHousewifeNicknameSheilaRelationshipsFamilyRob Stanton(husband; deceased)Emily Stanton(daughter; deceased)Aunt Lydia(possible aunt, sister, aunt-in-law or sister-in-law)Elsie Warner(illegitimate step-daughter)RomancesRob Stanton† His WidowFriendsSheila MosconiformerlyLeo MosconiformerlyApril WarnerMary VlasinEnemiesRalph Vlasin†PortrayalPortrayerGinnifer GoodwinHartlyn Hilsman(Young Beth Ann)Seasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeBeth Ann Stantonis one of the main characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byGinnifer Goodwin. On first blush, Beth Ann might strike you as the quintessential housewife from 1963. she's wearing a mask to hide her pain. Beth Ann's whole world revolves around her husband, and so she is understandably defensive and immediately in denial when toldRobis having an affair. Beth Ann will be determined to learn the sordid details of her husband's dirty deeds—and will be in for a life-changing shift of perspective.

characters.name: Catherine Castillo
characters.description: Catherine CastilloBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyCarlo Castillo(late father)Rita Castillo(step-mother)RomancesHank (ex-fiancé)Scooter Polarsky(former)EnemiesRita CastilloPortrayalPortrayerVeronica FalcónSeasons2First AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Unguarded MomentCatherine Castillois one of the main characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byVeronica Falcón. She isCarlo Castillo’s spinster daughter. Prim and proper, Catherine detests her father’s gorgeous wife,Rita, and returns to town after a mysterious accident befalls her father, seeking a way to destroy her.

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: ClaireBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationSecretaryNicknameClaireRelationshipsRomancesRob Stanton(ex-affair)PortrayalPortrayerLindsey KraftSeasons1First AppearanceI Killed Everyone He Did, But Backwards and In High HeelsClaireis one of the recurring characters onCBS All Access'Why Women Kill. She is portrayed byLindsey Kraft. She is Rob's secretary. She was the one that left the gate open at the Stanton's old house, their daughter Emily would then wander off through the gate and onto the street where she was hit by a car and died. Claire was a soft spoken woman when she worked forRobas his secretary. She concealed his affairs from his wifeBeth Ann Stantonso well that Beth never suspected a thing.  Not much else is known about her. When she told Beth Ann about the truth about the incident and Emily’s death, she cried and beg for forgiveness from Beth Ann, showing remorse for her actions.

CannonSeasons2First AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Lady ConfessesDeirdra "Dee" Fillcot(now Loomis) is one of the main characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byB.K. Cannon. She isAlma’s wry and brassy daughter who works as a waitress in a local diner. Though self-deprecating, Dee can be vulnerable under her emotional armour and finds her illicit affair with a secret lover to be both exciting and humiliating.

characters.name: Duke Riley
characters.description: Duke RileyBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusDeadCause of DeathStabbed to death (by Jade)RelationshipsRomancesJade(ex-girlfriend)EnemiesJadeTaylor HardingEli CohenPortrayalPortrayerKevin McNamaraSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceYou Had Me at HomicideI Was Just Wondering What Makes Dames Like You So DeadlyDukeis one of the recurring characters onCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byKevin McNamara. Duke is a dangerous man. While he doesn’t take crap from anyone, he can back down when provoked, unless he’s pushed too far or is determined to get what he wants. Not much is known about Duke’s background, other than he is a violent two-time felon.

characters.name: Eli Cohen
characters.description: Eli CohenBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationScreenwriterRelationshipsFamilyJake Cohen (father)Taylor Harding(wife)Unnamed Mother-in-lawVelma (sister-in-law)Unnamed Sister-in-lawRomancesJade† (ex-girlfriend)FriendsLamarEnemiesDuke Riley†PortrayalPortrayerReid ScottSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeEli Cohenis one of the main characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byReid Scott. Eli is Jewish, good-natured and smart enough to know that his sense of humor is the sexiest thing about him. He is a stay-at-home writer, married toTaylor, whom he finds alluring and exciting, constantly impressed by her fearlessness. He’s happy with their unconventional marriage, pleased that both have the liberty to sleep with other people as long as they don’t bring their playmates home. However, all that is about to change when Eli allows Taylor to let her lesbian lover crash with them for the weekend. Eli is a genuinely nice and easy-going man who appreciates strong women. He's both open to compromise and needling since he's not the type who wants to argue. Unfortunately, this also leads to him being easily manipulatable.

characters.name: Gloria
characters.description: GloriaBiographical InformationGenderFemaleCharacter InformationOccupationProstituteRelationshipsRomancesCarlo Castillo(former)Gloriawas another prostitute at the same brothel asRita.Carlo Castillowas "sweet on her" untilIsabelframed her for the theft of his watch. She never appears on screen, but is mentioned in the episodeMurder, My Sweet.

characters.name: Grace Berk
characters.description: Grace BerkBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyAunt Enid (deceased)RomancesJoan(lover)HusbandFriendsRita CastilloAlma Fillcot(former)MavisEnemiesAlma FillcotPortrayalPortrayerVirginia WilliamsFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Lady ConfessesGrace Berkis one of the secondary characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byVirginia Williams. Grace is a member of theElysian Park Garden Club. GRAH ! Grace is portrayed as a kind, gentle person who genuinely cares about those around her. This is seen throughout the series; for example, when she repeatedly attempts to getAlmain theGarden Club, warns her ofRita'ssurprise visit, and continues to talk to Rita after the rest of the Garden Club Ladies have cast her out. Grace is portrayed as a kind, gentle person who genuinely cares about those around her. This is seen throughout the series; for example, when she repeatedly attempts to getAlmain theGarden Club, warns her ofRita'ssurprise visit, and continues to talk to Rita after the rest of the Garden Club Ladies have cast her out. Grace is, however, similar to the other Garden Club Ladies in certain respects, namely in her love of gossip and underhanded remarks, though she is shown to be far less vicious than the rest of them.

characters.name: Isabel Vega
characters.description: Isabel VegaBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathInjection (byAlma Fillcot, also killed same way asCarlo Castillo) †Character InformationOccupationMaid (former)RelationshipsFamilyRita Castillo(cousin) †PortrayalPortrayerEileen GalindoFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorMurder, My SweetIsabel Vegais one of the secondary characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byEileen Galindo. Isabel is employed byRitaandCarlo Castilloas a maid for the majority of the series. Isabel convinced her cousinRitato leave her abusivehusband. When he came home before they could escape, he grabbed Rita's broken arm and threatened to beat her with his belt, but Isabel shot him in the back before he could. Believeing him to be dead, both women fled to start a new life in Los Angeles. While Rita worked as a prostitute, it is unclear exactly what Isabel's role was - perhaps a maid at the brothel, due to her differing attire to Rita in the flashback scene. Whatever her purpose, she had the opportunity to steal the watch of wealthy patronCarlo Castilloand frame his current lover. She sold the watch to buy Rita a new dress, and instructed her to seduce Carlo so they could both live off of his money. The plan worked and Rita married Carlo, but Isabel was only welcome in the house as their maid.

Believeing him to be dead, both women fled to start a new life in Los Angeles. While Rita worked as a prostitute, it is unclear exactly what Isabel's role was - perhaps a maid at the brothel, due to her differing attire to Rita in the flashback scene. Whatever her purpose, she had the opportunity to steal the watch of wealthy patronCarlo Castilloand frame his current lover. She sold the watch to buy Rita a new dress, and instructed her to seduce Carlo so they could both live off of his money. The plan worked and Rita married Carlo, but Isabel was only welcome in the house as their maid.

characters.name: Jade
characters.description: Jade/IreneBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathStabbed in the stomach (byTaylor)Character InformationNicknameIrene Tabatchnick (real name, under which crimes were committed)RelationshipsFamilyVerna Roy † (foster mother)Unnamed Foster Father †RomancesDuke Riley† (ex-boyfriend)Taylor Harding(ex-girlfriend)Eli Cohen(ex-boyfriend)Willow (ex-lover)Mischa (ex-lover)FriendsWillowMischaEnemiesDuke Riley †Taylor HardingEli CohenPortrayalPortrayerAlexandra DaddarioSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeJade(real nameIrene Tabatchnick) is one of the main characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byAlexandra Daddario. Jade is stunningly beautiful, also sweet, she isTaylor’s bisexual lover, extremely grateful to Taylor andElifor letting her stay with them after being stalked by adangerous ex. A neat freak who cleans compulsively, she impresses Eli who is thrilled to have his slacks and shirts ironed and the house kept in immaculate order. But neither Taylor nor Eli suspect that Jade may be a little vicious. Irene is from Ohio. She was thrown out of three foster homes by the time she turned 16. The first two times were for being caught stealing money. The third time was for when her foster mother, Verna Roy, accused Irene of trying to seduce her husband. A week later, Verna and her husband’s house burned down, killing them both. Irene disappeared soon after, changing her name to Jade and moving to Pasadena.

characters.name: Joan
characters.description: JoanBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyHusbandSonRomancesGrace Berk(lover)HusbandFriendsRita Castillo(former)Alma Fillcot(former)MavisEnemiesAlma FillcotRita CastilloPortrayalPortrayerJessica PhillipsFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceSecret Beyond The DoorThe Unguarded MomentJoanis one of the secondary characters in thesecond seasonofParamount+'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byJessica Phillips. Joan is a member of theElysian Park Garden Club. Joan is having an affair withGrace Berk. They meet at the Sunset Palace Hotel at 1:30pm sharp every Tuesday and Friday.Ritahas known of this affair for some time, and used it to keep them both in line for an unspecified amount of time. WhenAlmadiscovers the affair, she blackmails Joan into dropping out of the running for president of theGarden Club, making Grace leave the club altogether. It is likely Joan leaves with her, although this is never confirmed. InThe Unguarded MomentJoan makes it clear that she despisesRita, implying that any friendly behaviour seen earlier in the series was merely a ploy to stay on her good side due to the compromising information she had. Alternatively, it could be that she is so disgusted by Rita's former life that her opinion of her changed entirely, but this seems more unlikely.

characters.name: Karl Grove
characters.description: Karl GroveBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusDeceasedCause of DeathEuthanised (bySimone)Character InformationOccupationArt Gallery OwnerRelationshipsFamilySimone Grove(wife)Amy Grove(stepdaughter)RomancesWinston (affair)Hector (affair)PortrayalPortrayerJack DavenportSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeKarl Groveis one of the main characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byJack Davenport. Karl seen in 1982. He is handsome, well-bred and “endlessly amused by his wife,”Simone, whose extravagance he indulges. He knows he’s a supporting player in his wife’s life but happy to play his part. Karl is devastated and defeated when Simone uncovers a part of his life that he has kept hidden from her. Karl is a serial adulterer who's a romantic at heart. He admires beauty, grace, and self-confidence. Personally, he is more sensitive than weak-willed even while he puts the airs of a charming, put-together man. It's less to say he has lovers and more like he does not wholly commit to one person. Becoming excited and interested in the next new person even as it whittles away and costs his own marriage to Simone.

characters.name: Leo Mosconi
characters.description: Leo MosconiBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilySheila Mosconi(wife)Benny Mosconi (son)Mikey MosconiTwo other unnamed childrenEnzo Narducci (nephew-in-law)FriendsBeth Ann StantonRob StantonPortrayalPortrayerAdam FerraraSeasons1First AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryLeo Mosconiis one of the recurring characters onCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byAdam Ferrara. He is married toSheilaand owner of a dry-cleaning chain in Brooklyn, set in 1963. Always good-humored and happy to welcomeRobandBeth Annto the neighborhood. Leo is an affable man who tries his best to keep the peace. While he seems to support his strong-willed wife personally, in social situations his first reaction is to avoid offending other parties. Leo is an affable man who tries his best to keep the peace. While he seems to support his strong-willed wife personally, in social situations his first reaction is to avoid offending other parties. Leo has a sharp memory and awareness of tact. When he realizes that Rob is a man he'd seen kissing a waitress in the parking lot of a diner, his first reaction was to send Sheila a look so they could subtly leave quickly to inform her.

characters.name: Mary Vlasin
characters.description: Mary VlasinBiographical InformationAgeUnknown, between 40 and 50GenderFemaleStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationHousewifeRelationshipsFamilyRalph Vlasin(husband; deceased)FriendsBeth Ann StantonEnemiesRob(possible enemy)Ralph Vlasin(abuser)PortrayalPortrayerLio TiptonSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearancePractically Lethal in Every WayKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeMary Vlasinis one of the recurring characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. She is portrayed byLio Tipton. Mary isRalph’s wife. She’s a quiet and sweet woman, who doesn’t talk to many people, especially men, for fear of her husband’s jealousy and violent temper. Mary is seen as a sweet and quiet housewife, happily married and living in a nice house with a loving husband. But behind doors, her husband Ralph is controlling and abusive.

characters.name: Naomi Harte
characters.description: Naomi HarteBiographical InformationGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyEdmond Harte † (husband)Tommy Harte(son)FriendsWandaJoyce DubnerEnemiesSimone GrovePortrayalPortrayerKatie FinneranSeasons1First AppearanceMurder Means Never Having to Say You’re SorryNaomi Harteis one of the recurring characters onCBS All Access'Why Women Kill. She is portrayed byKatie Finneran. She is upscale, brash and fun, she is one ofSimone’s friends andTommy’s mother. Effusive, she is a cheerful widow, left $6 million when her husband, Ed, died, which she claims was “pretty nice.” Naomi: Simone! The house. It's finally done.Simone: My decorator finished Friday. What do you think?Naomi: [gaps] I'm so jealous I could kill myself.Simone: Oh. That's the look I was going for.[they both laugh as Karl appears]Karl: Darling, I got you a scotch.Simone: Oh Thank you, my love. Don't I have the nicest husband?Naomi: I don't know. Ed died and left me six million. That was pretty nice.

characters.name: Ralph Vlasin
characters.description: Ralph VlasinBiographical InformationGenderMaleStatusDeceasedDate of Death1973Cause of DeathCapital PunishmentRelationshipsFamilyMary Vlasin(wife)EnemiesRob StantonBeth Ann StantonPortrayalPortrayerScott PorterSeasons1First AppearanceLast AppearancePractically Lethal in Every WayKill Me as if It Were the Last TimeRalph Vlasinis one of the recurring characters in thefirst seasonofCBS All Access'sWhy Women Kill. He is portrayed byScott Porter. Ralph isMary’s abusive and controlling husband, who gets easily jealous if he sees her talking to other men, and isn’t afraid of confrontation, no matter where and when. Ralph appears to be a nice guy, happily married, and living in a nice house with a good job. But behind doors, Ralph is a controlling and abusive man with a violent temper, hitting or becoming aggressive towards his wife, Mary. And very aggressively rude.
